title: gwilymia coriacea
intro: Gwilymia coriacea, also known as the Australian saltbush, is a shrub native to Australia and is a member of the plant family Amaranthaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Gwilymia coriacea include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and soothe digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Gwilymia coriacea can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and can be used to support overall health and well-being.
